# Figure 4 — Methods summary (auto-generated 2026-04-25)

This file is auto-generated by scripts/11_methods_summary.py from the
processed data files. Every value below traces to a CSV in
data_processed/.

## Year window
  All FAOSTAT layers averaged over 2018–2020 (2018-2020 mean).
  Soil layers (SoilGrids 2.0 v2.0.0) are time-invariant within this
  analytical window.

## Buffer proxy (panel a / country buffer side)
  Source: SoilGrids 2.0 5 km aggregated rasters (Homolosine, ESRI:54052)
    - ocs_0-30cm_mean   (SOC stock 0–30 cm; t C / ha)
    - nitrogen 0-5, 5-15, 15-30 cm (cg/kg → ÷100 → g/kg)
    - bdod     0-5, 5-15, 15-30 cm (cg/cm³ → ÷100 → g/cm³)

  Total N stock 0–30 cm computed by integration over depth slabs:
    N_stock_slab [t N/ha] = N_conc [g/kg] × BD [g/cm³] × thickness [m] × 10

  Country aggregation: PHASE 2 LOCKED — cropland-weighted mean.
    Weights: MIRCA2000 maximum total annual cropped area (Portmann et al.
    2010; Zenodo 7422506). 5 arcmin (~9 km) ESRI-ASCII grid resampled to
    SoilGrids 5 km Homolosine via rasterio Resampling.sum.
    buffer_country_cwm = Σ(buffer × cropped_area) / Σ(cropped_area)
  Phase 1 (median, all-land) values retained in buffer_country.csv as
  cross-check fields with `_allland_` suffix.

  Buffer proxy = country-cropland-weighted-mean total N stock 0–30 cm
               (mathematically equal to SOC_stock / C:N within cropland)

  Pre-specified terciles within cropland-bearing countries (≥ 100,000 ha):
    Low   buffer: < 6.53 t N/ha
    High  buffer: ≥ 9.30 t N/ha
    countries entering tercile calculation: 154

## Exposure (panel b / country exposure side)
  Sources: FAOSTAT bulk CSVs
    - Inputs / Fertilizers by Nutrient (Item: Nutrient nitrogen N (total);
      Elements: Agricultural Use, Production, Import quantity,
                Export quantity)
    - Inputs / Land Use (Item: Cropland; Element: Area; unit 1000 ha)

  Sub-index 1 — Synthetic N application intensity:
    N_intensity_raw    = N_use_t × 1000 / cropland_ha   [kg N / ha]
    N_intensity_scaled = min(N_intensity_raw, 300) / 300   [0–1]

  Sub-index 2 — Net N-fertilizer import reliance:
    Apparent_consumption = Production + Imports − Exports   [t N]
    Net_imports          = max(0, Imports − Exports)        [t N]
    Import_reliance      = clip(Net_imports / Apparent_consumption, 0, 1)

  Combined index (kept for SI cross-check only):
    Exposure = 0.5×N_intensity_scaled + 0.5×Import_reliance

## Exposure classification — LOCKED Modified Two-Pathway Max-Rule
  HIGH if either pathway holds:
    (A) input-intensity     : N_intensity_raw ≥ 150 kg N/ha
    (B) import-dependence   : Import_reliance ≥ 0.70 AND N_intensity_raw ≥ 25 kg N/ha
  LOW  if N_intensity_raw < 50 AND Import_reliance < 0.30
  MODERATE: everything else
  DATA_MISSING: intensity or reliance unavailable

## Re-export hub flag (tightened, not a classification)
  All three must hold:
    (Imports + Exports) / Apparent_consumption > 3
    Imports + Exports > 50000 t N
    Apparent_consumption > 10000 t N
  Treatment: flagged in data table + caption footnote; no map hatching.

## Panel c — Combined buffer × exposure classification (5 classes + missing)
  Class assignments:
    high buffer  × low  exposure  → 'High buffer / low exposure'
    high buffer  × high exposure  → 'High buffer / high exposure'
    low  buffer  × low  exposure  → 'Low buffer / low exposure'
    low  buffer  × high exposure  → 'Low buffer / high exposure'  (focal)
    any moderate component        → 'Intermediate'
    missing input                 → 'Data missing'

## Final composition (cropland-area-weighted)
    intermediate                       948.3 Mha    60.5 %
    low_buffer_high_exposure           272.1 Mha    17.4 %
    high_buffer_low_exposure           186.9 Mha    11.9 %
    low_buffer_low_exposure             82.2 Mha     5.2 %
    high_buffer_high_exposure           61.4 Mha     3.9 %
    data_missing                        15.6 Mha     1.0 %

## Stake-floor sensitivity (focal-class cropland share)
    Rule                                          focal-class %
    combined index ≥ 0.50                           15.1 %
    combined index ≥ 0.66 (original)                 0.0 %
    exposure terciles                                6.3 %
    modified max-rule (stake=10)                    19.2 %
    modified max-rule (stake=25) *LOCKED*           17.4 %
    modified max-rule (stake=50)                    14.0 %
    raw max-rule (no stake floor)                   26.7 %

## Scope statement (must appear in main text and SI)
  This figure maps a mechanism-specific resilience screen. It does not
  include poverty, irrigation, crop mix, conflict, fiscal capacity,
  food-import dependence, or adaptive policy response, and should not
  be interpreted as a food-security vulnerability index.

## Phase 2 limitations (remaining)
  1. Panel a is a country-level cropland-weighted choropleth, not a
     pixel-level cropland raster. Pixel-level panel a would require
     SoilGrids 250 m tile mosaic + cropland mask (~5 GB). Country-level
     panel a is publication-grade for the figure's altitude.
  2. MIRCA2000 cropped-area weights are circa 2000. Newer 5 arcmin
     cropland products (e.g., MIRCA-OS, ESA WorldCover) could be used
     in a future revision; for now, MIRCA is openly available without
     registration and gives reasonable cropland weights. Cropland
     extent has shifted somewhat 2000→2020 (esp. tropical expansion).
  3. N intensity uses cropland denominator, while FAOSTAT 'Agricultural
     Use' includes fertilizer applied to permanent pasture. Pasture-
     heavy countries (Ireland, NZ, UK, Australia) get inflated raw
     intensity; the 300 kg/ha cap absorbs the worst cases. SI should
     report sensitivity using Cropland + Permanent meadows as the
     denominator.
